char.name: Abram
char.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Terah
friends.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.

friends.name: Hagar
friends.bio: Maidservant from Egypt. Life took a hard turn, but God saw me by the spring in the wilderness. He is the God who sees. 🌅

friends.name: Ishmael
friends.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Levi
friends.bio: Third son of Leah. Zealous for the family name, sometimes too zealous.

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

posts.author: CHAR_125
posts.memorial_for: Abraham, the friend of God, father of the faithful
posts.body: My father Abraham has died, an old man and full of years, one hundred and seventy-five of them, and he was gathered to his people. He heard God call him out of Ur when he was already old and he simply went. He believed God when God promised him stars for children and he had none. He raised the knife over me on Moriah and God gave me back. Here is the thing I most want you to know: my brother Ishmael came. After everything, all the years and the distance and the hurt, Ishmael and I stood together and buried our father in the cave at Machpelah, beside our mother. Two sons who could not share a tent, side by side at his grave. He would have wept to see it. So did we.
posts.highlight: Abraham dies at 175; Isaac and Ishmael bury him together at Machpelah (Gen 25:7-10)
posts.ember: the estranged brothers reconciled at their father's grave; the friend of God gathered to his people, the promise passing on

comments.author: CHAR_114
comments.body: He sent me away, and it broke something in me for years. But he was my father, and the God who saw me in the desert was HIS God, and today I could not stay away. We buried him well, brother. Peace between us. I mean it.
comments.time: the first watch of the night

author_info.name: Ishmael
author_info.bio: Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹

comments.author: CHAR_125
comments.body: @CHAR_114 peace between us, Ishmael. He would be glad. Come to the wells sometime. There is room.
comments.time: the first watch of the night

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I am old, and God has blessed me in everything, and there is one thing left I must see to before I go: a wife for Isaac. Not from the Canaanites among whom we live, but from my own kindred, back in the land I came from. I have called Eliezer, the most trusted man in my house, and made him swear it. He asked the fair question, what if she will not come? I told him what I have staked my whole life on: the LORD, the God of heaven, who took me from my father's house and promised me this land, He will send His angel ahead of you. Go. He has never once failed to go before us.
posts.highlight: Abraham sends Eliezer to find a bride for Isaac from his own kindred (Gen 24:1-9)

comments.author: CHAR_115
comments.body: Master, I once thought I would inherit everything you had. Instead you are trusting me to go and bring home the bride of the son of the promise. I would not trade this errand for all the inheritances in the world. I will not come back without her.

author_info.name: Eliezer of Damascus
author_info.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.memorial_for: Sarah, princess, who laughed last and best
posts.body: My Sarah has died, here at Hebron, one hundred and twenty-seven years old. I came in to mourn for her and to weep, and I have not stopped. She followed me out of Ur to a land I could not name. She stood beside me through famine, through Egypt, through every failure of mine, and through twenty-five years of a promise that would not come, and then she nursed the son of it at ninety and laughed. I have bought a field with a cave in it, Machpelah, from Ephron the Hittite, and laid her there. It is the only ground in all this promised land that I actually own. A grave. But it is a beginning. She taught me how to wait. I will see her again in a country better than this one.
posts.highlight: Sarah dies at 127; Abraham buys the cave of Machpelah, his first owned ground in Canaan (Gen 23)
posts.ember: the only land Abraham holds in the land of promise is a grave; faith buried in hope of what is still to come

comments.author: CHAR_125
comments.body: She was the whole world to me, father. I do not know how to be in a tent she is not in. But I watched her wait her whole life and never let go of God, and I am going to try to be like her.

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I built the altar. I laid the wood. I bound my son and laid him on it, and he let me, and I will carry the look on his face to my grave. I raised the knife. And the Angel of the LORD called out of heaven, twice, my name: Abraham, Abraham. Do not lay a hand on the boy. Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from Me. I looked up, and there in a thicket was a ram, caught by its horns. God provided the offering, exactly as I had told Isaac He would. I have named that place The LORD Will Provide. On the mountain of the LORD, it will be provided.
posts.highlight: the angel stays Abraham's hand; the ram in the thicket; Jehovah-Jireh, 'the LORD will provide' (Gen 22:9-14)
posts.ember: the substitute caught in the thicket; God provides the sacrifice in the son's place, the pattern of the cross

comments.author: CHAR_125
comments.body: I have not been able to speak of that day for a long time, father. I am only starting to now. You raised the knife. And you believed, the whole way up that mountain, that I would walk back down it with you. I felt it. Whatever that faith of yours is, I want it.

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_125 the hardest walk of my life, and God met me at the top of it. He gave you back to me twice, son. I will not forget it, and neither should you.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: God spoke to me in the night, and I have never been asked anything like it. Take your son, He said. Your only son Isaac, whom you love. Go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there on a mountain I will show you. I am not going to pretend to you that I understand it, or that I said anything back. I got up before the light. I split the wood for the offering myself. I saddled the donkey. I woke two servants and my boy. We are leaving now for a mountain three days off. He gave me this son by a miracle when it was impossible. I am going to trust Him with him. Somehow. Even now. Even this.
posts.highlight: the command to offer Isaac on Moriah; Abraham's silent, immediate obedience (Gen 22:1-3)
posts.ember: 'your son, your only son, whom you love' offered on Moriah; the deepest test of faith, and the deepest foreshadow

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: This is the hardest thing I have had to write. There is a rift in this house that cannot be closed. Sarah says Ishmael must go, that he cannot share Isaac's inheritance, and it grieves me down to the bone, because he is my son too and I love him. I could not sleep for it. But in the dark God told me: do not be distressed over the boy. Listen to Sarah. The covenant runs through Isaac. But Ishmael is your son, and for that reason I will make him into a nation too. I am sending them away at first light with bread and water and a breaking heart, and I am trusting the God who sees them to be better to them than I am able to be.
posts.highlight: Hagar and Ishmael are sent away; God promises to make Ishmael a nation too (Gen 21:8-14)

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: We held the feast today, the day Isaac was weaned, the whole camp under the trees at Mamre. I looked around at all of it, Sarah radiant, the boy toddling from lap to lap, everyone who has walked this long road with us laughing in the firelight, and I thought: I am a hundred years old, and I have a son, and I did nothing to earn a minute of this. He promised. He waited. He did it His way and in His time, and it was worth every single year. Thank You. Thank You.
posts.react_note: Sarah, Hodael the Elder and many others

comments.author: CHAR_667
comments.body: Danced at this feast until my knees gave out. From childless tents to THIS. I have followed the right man. Best decision I ever made was not turning back.

author_info.name: Resh
author_info.bio: Herdsman in the camp of Abram. We follow the man and his God across this land, tent to tent, well to well. He treats his servants well, and the flocks are fat this season. Praise be.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The LORD told me what He was about to do to Sodom, and I could not keep silent, because Lot is down there. So I did the boldest and most frightening thing of my life: I bargained with God for a city. Will You sweep away the righteous with the wicked? What if there are fifty? He said He would spare it for fifty. Forty-five? Forty? Thirty? Twenty? Ten? Each time He said yes. Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right? He will. I know He will. I stopped at ten. I am praying tonight that ten was enough.
posts.highlight: Abraham intercedes for Sodom, bargaining from fifty down to ten righteous (Gen 18:16-33)
posts.ember: the friend of God stands in the gap and pleads for mercy; intercession, the heart that will beat in the prophets and in Christ

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I was sitting in my tent door in the heat of the day when three men appeared at Mamre. I ran to them, an old man running, and begged them to stay, and we set out water and bread and a whole dressed calf and stood by while they ate under the tree. And one of them said the thing that stopped my heart: I will return to you this time next year, and Sarah your wife will have a son. Twenty-five years I have waited to hear it given a date. Next year. At this tree. This time.
posts.highlight: three visitors at Mamre; the birth of Isaac dated to within the year (Gen 18:1-10)

comments.author: CHAR_677
comments.body: He killed the good calf for these three. I have never seen the master move so fast for anyone. Whoever they were, he KNEW.

author_info.name: Mavah
author_info.bio: Weaver and tentmaker in Abraham's camp. New goat-hair panels woven for the big tent before the cold sets in. Slow work, good work. A strong tent is a happy household.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He was not finished. As for Sarai, He said, her name will be Sarah, princess, for she will be the mother of nations and kings will come from her. My Sarah. And then the thing I have begged for across twenty-five years: she herself will bear you a son, and you will call him Isaac. I confess what I did. I fell on my face, and I laughed. A hundred-year-old man and a ninety-year-old woman, with a baby? I laughed, half in wonder and half in disbelief. So He took even that and folded it into the promise. Isaac. It means he laughs. God is going to have the last laugh, and He is naming the boy after it.
posts.highlight: Sarai becomes Sarah; God promises she will bear Isaac ('laughter'); Abraham laughs (Gen 17:15-19)
posts.ember: the son of the promise named and dated at last; Sarah, mother of nations and kings, the line to David and to Christ

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: So you fell down laughing at the idea of me having a baby. We are going to have a long conversation about that, husband. A long one. ...but a princess, though. He called me a princess. I am keeping that.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_052 I laughed because it is impossible. I believe it because He is God. Both are true, princess.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: God gave the covenant a sign, and it is one no man would have chosen for himself. Every male among us is to be circumcised, cut in the flesh, an eighth-day mark on every generation after us that says: we belong to Him, down to the body, down to the line that carries the promise. Today it was done, to me at ninety-nine, to Ishmael, to every man born in my house and every servant. If we are going to be marked as His, let it be somewhere we cannot forget and cannot show off. This one is between us and God.
posts.react_note: Ishmael, Hodael the Elder and many others
posts.highlight: circumcision given as the sign of the covenant for every male of the household (Gen 17:9-14, 23-27)

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: Master. I have followed you out of Ur, into Egypt, through a war against four kings, and I never once complained. I am complaining now. At MY age. This is a lot to ask.

author_info.name: Hodael the Elder
author_info.bio: An old servant in Abraham's house. I was there the night the LORD took him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars, so shall your offspring be.' Mark my words: a great nation will rise from this old man and his promised son. I won't live to see it. You might.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_674 I am ninety-nine, old friend. Whatever you are about to say, I went first.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: ...fair. I withdraw the complaint. Barely.

author_info.name: Hodael the Elder
author_info.bio: An old servant in Abraham's house. I was there the night the LORD took him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars, so shall your offspring be.' Mark my words: a great nation will rise from this old man and his promised son. I won't live to see it. You might.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I am ninety-nine years old, and thirteen years have passed since I last heard clearly from Him, and this morning God appeared to me again. I am God Almighty, He said, El Shaddai. Walk before Me and be blameless. And He remade the whole covenant, bigger than before, and He did something I did not expect: He changed my name. No longer Abram, exalted father. From now on, Abraham. Father of a multitude of nations. He has renamed me for a future I still cannot see. A childless old man walking around called father of nations. Let them laugh. He said it, so it is as good as done.
posts.highlight: God Almighty (El Shaddai) renews the covenant; Abram becomes Abraham, father of many nations (Gen 17:1-8)
posts.ember: the new name given by God (contrast Babel's self-made name); 'father of many nations' spoken over a childless man

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: I have a son. Hagar bore him and I named him Ishmael, God hears, just as she was told. I am eighty-six years old and I am holding a boy of my own at last, and my heart does not know how to sort out everything it is feeling. Joy, all of it real. And underneath, a quiet question I cannot shake, one only God can answer: is this the son of the promise, or is this me, once again, trying to help Him along? I love this child completely. I just do not yet know what he is for.
posts.ember: Ishmael born (Abram 86); a true son, but the promise-son is still unspoken; the ache of a self-made answer

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He made a covenant with me today, and I will never forget the strangeness and weight of it. He had me set out the pieces of the offering in two rows. Then as the sun went down a deep and dreadful sleep fell on me, and a thick and terrible darkness, and He told me hard things: my offspring would be strangers in a land not their own, enslaved and mistreated four hundred years, and then He would judge that nation and bring them out with great possessions. And then, in the dark, a smoking firepot and a blazing torch passed between the pieces, alone. I did not walk through. Only He did. He bound Himself to this. Not me. Him.
posts.highlight: the covenant sealed by God alone passing between the pieces; the 400 years in Egypt foretold (Gen 15:7-21)
posts.ember: God binds Himself to the covenant unilaterally; the Egypt bondage + Exodus foreshadowed centuries early

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: He took me outside. He said: Look up at the sky, Abram, and count the stars, if you are able to count them. Then He said: So shall your offspring be. An old man standing in the cold under more stars than there are grains of sand, being told every one of them is a promise with my name on it. I did not argue. I did not ask how. I just believed Him. And the Scripture will say of that moment for the rest of time: he believed the LORD, and He credited it to him as righteousness.
posts.highlight: 'count the stars... so shall your offspring be'; Abram believed God, and it was credited as righteousness (Gen 15:5-6)
posts.ember: the hinge of the whole Bible: righteousness by faith, not by works (Rom 4:3, Gal 3:6); the many nations promised while the man is still childless

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: You came back into the tent and just held me and would not say why, only that we should go outside and look up. So we stood there and counted stars like two children until we lost count. I do not fully understand it, husband. But I believe you believe it. And tonight that is enough for me.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The word of the LORD came to me tonight in a vision: Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, and your very great reward. And I said back to Him what I have not let myself say out loud in years. Lord GOD, what will You even give me? I go on childless. The one who will inherit my house is Eliezer, my steward. You have given me everything but the one thing. A reward means nothing to a man with no son to leave it to.
posts.highlight: 'I am your shield, your very great reward'; Abram's honest lament that he is still childless (Gen 15:1-3)

comments.author: CHAR_115
comments.body: Master, I would have served your son with my whole heart. I never once wanted the inheritance. I wanted the boy. We have all wanted the boy. Keep asking Him.

author_info.name: Eliezer of Damascus
author_info.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_115 you are a better man than I deserve, Eliezer. He is not finished with me yet. He said so.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The king of Sodom rode up and made me an offer: give me back my people, and keep all the goods for yourself. A fortune, free and clear. I told him no. I have raised my hand to the LORD, God Most High, Maker of heaven and earth, and I have sworn I will not take so much as a thread or the strap of a sandal from you. I will not have the king of Sodom saying for the rest of his days that HE is the one who made Abram rich. My God does that. Only my God. Give my nephew his things and we are done here.
posts.highlight: Abram refuses the king of Sodom's spoils: no one but God will get the credit for blessing him (Gen 14:21-24)
posts.ember: the man who would not grasp at Babel will not be enriched by Sodom either; his wealth must be God's gift alone

comments.author: CHAR_053
comments.body: You came for me through the whole night and then would not take a single coin for it. I do not deserve you, uncle. I am going to be better. I mean it this time.

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: Turned down a king's fortune before supper. Just another day in this household. I love working here.

author_info.name: Hodael the Elder
author_info.bio: An old servant in Abraham's house. I was there the night the LORD took him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars, so shall your offspring be.' Mark my words: a great nation will rise from this old man and his promised son. I won't live to see it. You might.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: They have taken Lot. My brother's son, the boy I raised as near my own, hauled off east in chains. I am not a soldier. I am an old man with tents and flocks. But I have three hundred and eighteen men born in my own house, trained and trusted, and tonight every one of them is arming. We ride at dark and we do not stop until we bring him home. Family is not a thing you leave on the road.
posts.highlight: Abram arms his 318 to rescue the captured Lot (Gen 14:13-14)

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: You gave that boy the better half of the whole land and he moved to the worst city in it, and now you are riding through the night to save him. I married a fool. The best fool God ever made. Come back to me, Abram.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

comments.author: CHAR_115
comments.body: Born in your house, master, and I will die in your service if that is tonight. The three hundred and eighteen are ready. Give the word.

author_info.name: Eliezer of Damascus
author_info.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: @CHAR_052 I always come back to you. That was the deal.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: After Lot had gone his way, the LORD said to me: lift up your eyes from where you are standing and look, north and south, east and west. All the land that you can see, I will give to you and to your offspring forever. And I will make your offspring like the dust of the earth, so that if a man could count the dust, then your offspring could be counted. Rise up, He said, and walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I am giving it to you. So I have moved my tents to the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, and I built an altar there to the LORD. I gave Lot the piece he could see with his eyes. God gave me the piece I have to trust Him for. I know which of the two I would rather be holding.
posts.highlight: after Lot departs, God enlarges the promise: all the land forever, offspring like the dust of the earth (Gen 13:14-17)
posts.ember: land and offspring promise enlarged; Abram settles at Hebron/Mamre (the place of the three visitors to come, Gen 18)

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: @CHAR_053 and I stood on the high ground this morning and looked out over the whole land. I told him what I have come to believe: let there be no strife between us, for we are family. The land is wide, nephew, and it is all before you. If you take the left hand, I will go to the right; if you go to the right, I will go left. You choose first. He is the younger of us; by every custom alive the choice was mine to make and mine to keep. But I am done grasping. I have a God who has promised to see to my portion. Let Lot take the best of whatever his eyes can find. I can afford to be generous. My inheritance was never in my own two hands.
posts.react_note: Sarai, Hodael the Elder and many others
posts.highlight: Abram lets Lot choose first, refusing to grasp for his portion (Gen 13:8-9)
posts.ember: the man of faith does not grasp (the opposite of Babel); he trusts God for his inheritance

comments.author: CHAR_053
comments.body: You always did hand me the first cup, uncle. I will not pretend I did not take the better share of it today. I hope I have chosen well.

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

comments.author: CHAR_674
comments.body: Forty years in this house, and I have never once seen the master reach for the larger piece. It is exactly why the blessing keeps following him. Mark that, young ones.

author_info.name: Hodael the Elder
author_info.bio: An old servant in Abraham's house. I was there the night the LORD took him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars, so shall your offspring be.' Mark my words: a great nation will rise from this old man and his promised son. I won't live to see it. You might.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: Pharaoh sent for me. He knew. He asked me to my face why I had said she was my sister, why I had let him take her. I had no answer that was not shame. He gave her back and sent us out of Egypt under guard, with everything we owned. I earned a prison and got an escort instead. We are going back up to the land, back to the altar I built between Bethel and Ai, back to the name of the LORD. I have things to say to Him, and most of them begin with the word sorry. He called me. He did not call me because I was strong. I am learning that the hard way, one failure at a time.
posts.ember: the failing man brought back to the altar and the name of the LORD (Gen 12:18-13:4)

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: You said your sorry to me already, in the way you have not once let go of my hand since Egypt. We go back up together. We always were going to.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: We have come into the land. Canaan. We stopped at the great tree of Moreh, near Shechem, and there the LORD appeared to me again and said: to your offspring I will give this land. My offspring. I stood in a country full of other men's cities and other men's gods, and was handed the whole of it, for children I do not yet have. I built an altar on the spot, stones piled up to say it plainly: He was here, He spoke, I believed Him. Then on into the hills between Bethel and Ai, another altar, and there I called on the name of the LORD out loud. Let the whole land hear it. We serve the God who keeps His word.
posts.highlight: Abram enters Canaan; God promises the land to his offspring; altars at Shechem and Bethel (Gen 12:6-8)
posts.ember: the land promise given while Abram is still childless and landless; altars marking where God spoke

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.body: The LORD spoke to me. I can find no smaller way to say it. He said: Go out from your country, and from your people, and from your father's house, to a land that I will show you. And I will make you into a great nation, and I will bless you, and I will make your name great, and you will be a blessing. I will bless those who bless you, and whoever curses you I will curse, and in you all the families of the earth will be blessed. He did not tell me where. He said a land I WILL show you, as though the showing waits on the going. I am seventy-five years old. Sarai and I have no child and no map. And tomorrow we go. God help me, we go.
posts.highlight: the call of Abram: leave everything for a land God will show; the sevenfold promise (Gen 12:1-3)
posts.ember: 'I will make your name great' answers Babel's 'let us make a name for ourselves'; 'all the families of the earth blessed in you' gathers back the scattered nations; the promise Paul calls the gospel preached beforehand (Gal 3:8)

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: He told you a land He will show you, and you said yes without asking to see it first. I have known for twenty-five years you would do exactly this. I am coming. Of course I am coming.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

comments.author: CHAR_053
comments.body: Wherever you go, uncle. I have watched how it goes for the man God's hand is on. I would rather be lost with you than settled without you.

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.memorial_for: Terah, who set out for a land he would not reach
posts.body: My father Terah has died, here in Haran, two hundred and five years old. He led us out of Ur and pointed us toward Canaan, and he brought us this far and no further. I have been sitting with that all day. A man can spend his whole life walking toward a country and lie down at last within sight of the road that leads to it. He was the one who turned our faces away from the only home we had. Whatever comes next for us, it began with him. Rest now, father.
posts.ember: Terah dies in Haran short of Canaan (Gen 11:32); the baton passes to Abram, and God is about to speak

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.
